title: Phaedo: The Death of Socrates
description: Socrates drinks the hemlock and dies discussing the immortality of the soul — the most famous death scene in Western literature.
introduction: Socrates drinks the hemlock and dies discussing the immortality of the soul — the most famous death scene in Western literature.

translation.text: Socrates said: 'I owe a cock to Asclepius; do not forget to pay the debt.' These were his last words.
